skill.name: ohmg
skill.description: Multi-agent orchestration framework that coordinates specialized AI agents (PM, Frontend, Backend, Debug) with Serena Memory for cross-agent state management.
skill.summary: Multi-agent orchestration framework with specialized domain agents and Serena Memory state management.
skill.author: supercent-io

security_audit.summary: All static findings evaluated as false positives. External commands flagged are markdown documentation showing CLI commands, not actual executions. Network URLs are localhost addresses. No cryptographic algorithms present. The skill is safe documentation for a legitimate multi-agent orchestration framework.

content.value_statement: Managing complex projects across multiple AI agents is fragmented and error-prone. This skill provides a unified orchestration layer with specialized agents, Serena Memory state sharing, and real-time observability dashboards.
actual_capabilities: Spawns specialized agents (PM, Frontend, Backend, Debug) for different project domains
Manages cross-agent state via Serena Memory in .serena/memories/
Provides terminal and web dashboards for real-time observability
Configures per-agent CLI mapping to different AI providers (Gemini, Claude, Codex)
Supports SSE bridging for stdio-to-http environments
Runs system diagnostics and repair via bunx oh-my-ag doctor
limitations: Requires Bun CLI and uv package manager for full installation
Agents must be explicitly spawned; no automatic task detection
Claude Code, Codex, and Gemini CLIs must be pre-installed for full functionality

use_cases.title: Complex Multi-Domain Projects
use_cases.description: Coordinate frontend, backend, and API development across specialized agents that share state via Serena Memory.

use_cases.title: Parallel AI Agent Workflows
use_cases.description: Spawn multiple AI agents in parallel sessions for concurrent task execution with cross-agent visibility.
use_cases.target_user: Team leads delegating tasks to multiple AI assistants

use_cases.title: Real-Time Project Observability
use_cases.description: Monitor agent activity and project status through terminal or web dashboards during active development.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Agent Spawning
prompt_templates.prompt: /setup - Configure the multi-agent environment for my project
prompt_templates.scenario: Initial setup when starting a new project

prompt_templates.title: Spawning a Backend Agent
prompt_templates.prompt: Use oh-my-ag agent:spawn backend "Implement user authentication API" session-01
prompt_templates.scenario: Delegating a specific backend task to a specialized agent

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Agent Coordination
prompt_templates.prompt: /coordinate - Plan and coordinate frontend and backend development for a new feature
prompt_templates.scenario: Planning complex features requiring multiple specialized agents

prompt_templates.title: Dashboard Monitoring
prompt_templates.prompt: Launch the web dashboard to monitor active agent sessions and project status
prompt_templates.scenario: Checking real-time progress across all running agents

output_examples.input: Spawn a backend agent to implement the user registration API
output_examples.output: Backend agent initialized in session-01. Serena Memory updated with task context. Agent ready to receive implementation details for user registration endpoint.
Web dashboard started at http://localhost:9847. Active sessions: 2. Serena Memory sync status: Connected. Agent activity feed streaming in real-time.
Workflow Guide activated. PM Agent decomposing checkout flow into tasks. Frontend Agent queued for UI components. Backend Agent queued for payment API. QA Agent configured for review.

best_practices: Run bunx oh-my-ag doctor after initial setup to verify all dependencies and CLI configurations
Configure agent_cli_mapping in user-preferences.yaml before spawning agents to ensure optimal routing
Use Serena Memory checkpoints for long-running tasks to enable cross-agent resume capability
anti_patterns: Spawning agents without first configuring CLI mappings may result in suboptimal routing
Skipping the doctor check can lead to missing dependencies causing agent failures
Running multiple conflicting agents in the same session without proper state management

faq.question: What AI CLIs are supported by this skill?
faq.answer: This skill supports Claude Code, Codex, and Gemini CLI. Configure mappings in user-preferences.yaml to route tasks to the appropriate agent type.

faq.question: How does Serena Memory work?
faq.answer: Serena Memory writes structured state to .serena/memories/ directory. All agents can read from this shared state for cross-agent coordination and continuity.

faq.question: What is required before first use?
faq.answer: Install Bun CLI and uv package manager. Run bunx oh-my-ag to start the interactive setup, then run bunx oh-my-ag doctor to verify the installation.

faq.question: Can I monitor multiple agent sessions simultaneously?
faq.answer: Yes. The web dashboard at http://localhost:9847 shows all active sessions, agent activity, and Serena Memory sync status in real-time.

faq.question: How do I configure which CLI each agent uses?
faq.answer: Edit .agent/config/user-preferences.yaml and set the agent_cli_mapping section. For example: backend: codex or pm: claude.

faq.question: What does the /coordinate command do?
faq.answer: The /coordinate command activates the Workflow Guide to plan complex multi-domain projects, spawning PM, Frontend, Backend, and QA agents as needed.
